note: Patient is a 31-year-old female with moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis (ICD-10: L20.9) affecting 35% BSA with EASI score of 28.4. Completed 8-week trial of triamcinolone 0.1% cream (inadequate response, BSA unchanged at 33%). Completed 12-week trial of tacrolimus 0.1% ointment (discontinued due to burning intolerance, documented visit 03/2024). Dermatology consulted and recommends dupilumab given failed topical therapies and significant impact on quality of life (DLQI 18). No active ocular herpes or conjunctivitis. No live vaccines pending.

58-year-old male with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (E11.9), stage 3a CKD (N18.3), and established CAD (I25.10) post-NSTEMI 2021. HbA1c 9.1% on current regimen. Failed metformin 1000mg BID x 6 months (GI intolerance — nausea and diarrhea, documented). Failed glipizide 10mg daily x 4 months — three hypoglycemic episodes requiring glucose administration, discontinued 01/2024. Endocrinology recommends GLP-1 agonist given cardiovascular risk profile and renal considerations. UACR 85 mg/g. eGFR 48.

41-year-old male requesting MRI lumbar spine (CPT 72148) for low back pain (M54.5) of 3-week duration. Pain rated 4/10 at rest. No radicular symptoms, no bowel or bladder dysfunction, no fever, no unexplained weight loss. No prior physical therapy trial documented. No prior imaging. No NSAIDs trialed per medication history.

67-year-old female with seropositive rheumatoid arthritis (M05.79), RF positive 148 IU/mL, anti-CCP positive >250 U/mL. DAS28 score 5.6 (high disease activity). Failed methotrexate 20mg weekly x 6 months — hepatotoxicity, LFTs 3.2x ULN, discontinued 11/2023. Failed hydroxychloroquine 400mg daily x 5 months — inadequate response, DAS28 unchanged at 5.4. Rheumatology recommends TNF inhibitor therapy. IGRA negative. CXR clear 01/2024. No active infections.

38-year-old male requesting hereditary cancer gene panel (CPT 81408). No personal cancer history. No first-degree relatives with cancer. Patient reports general health anxiety and read about genetic testing online. No genetic counseling completed. No referral from oncology or genetics. Does not meet NCCN or payer criteria for hereditary cancer syndrome testing.

49-year-old female with treatment-resistant major depressive disorder (F33.2). PHQ-9 score 21 (severe). Failed sertraline 200mg x 10 weeks (no response), escitalopram 20mg x 8 weeks (partial response, PHQ-9 18), venlafaxine 225mg x 14 weeks (intolerance — hypertensive urgency, BP 188/112). No active substance use disorder per AUDIT-C. Psychiatrist at REMS-certified clinic requesting esketamine. Columbia Suicide Severity Rating Scale: moderate ideation, no plan or intent. Current safety plan in place.

34-year-old female requesting laparoscopic Roux-en-Y gastric bypass (CPT 43644). BMI 43.7 kg/m2 with morbid obesity (E66.01). Comorbidities: OSA confirmed by polysomnography (AHI 28, moderate-severe) (G47.33), hypertension on amlodipine 10mg and lisinopril 20mg (I10). Completed 6-month supervised weight loss program per Molina requirement — monthly visit documentation submitted. Psychological clearance obtained from licensed psychologist 02/2024. Nutritional counseling x 4 sessions completed. No medical contraindications identified by bariatric surgery team.

29-year-old female with severe persistent asthma (J45.51) requesting dupilumab. Currently on albuterol PRN only. No documented trial of inhaled corticosteroid (ICS). No ICS/LABA combination therapy trialed. FEV1 not documented in past 12 months. Eosinophil count not on file. No pulmonology referral. Two ED visits for asthma in past year noted but no outpatient asthma management plan.

74-year-old male with stage IV metastatic NSCLC, left upper lobe primary (C34.12). PD-L1 TPS 82%. No EGFR mutation. No ALK rearrangement. No ROS1 rearrangement. ECOG performance status 1. Oncology tumor board reviewed 02/14/2024 and approved pembrolizumab monotherapy as first-line treatment per KEYNOTE-024 data. Creatinine 1.1, LFTs WNL. No active autoimmune disease. Pulmonary function adequate. Patient counseled on immune-related adverse events and enrolled in Keytruda REMS.

52-year-old female with Type 2 diabetes (E11.65) and obesity (E66.09), BMI 38.4. HbA1c 8.7%. On metformin 2000mg daily (tolerated, partial response — HbA1c was 10.1% at initiation). Requesting tirzepatide for dual benefit of glycemic control and weight reduction. Endocrinology note supports. ASCVD risk score 18%. No prior GLP-1 or GIP agonist trial. No prior SGLT-2 inhibitor trial documented.

45-year-old male with psoriatic arthritis (M07.60) and plaque psoriasis (L40.50). PASI score 18.2, BSA 28%, DAS28 4.8. Failed methotrexate 15mg weekly x 6 months — nausea and oral ulcers, inadequate response (PASI unchanged). Failed sulfasalazine 1g BID x 4 months — inadequate joint response. Rheumatology and dermatology both support IL-17A inhibitor. No prior TNF inhibitor tried — requesting IL-17 first line given predominantly skin involvement and prior GI intolerance to methotrexate. Negative TB screening.

55-year-old male with low back pain with left-sided radiculopathy (M54.42) radiating to L4-L5 dermatomal distribution. Positive straight leg raise at 40 degrees. Foot drop noted on neurological exam (weakness 3/5 left EHL). Duration 8 weeks. No improvement with 6 weeks of PT (10 sessions documented). Ibuprofen 600mg TID trialed — partial response only. Requesting MRI lumbar spine prior to neurosurgery referral.

32-year-old male with schizophrenia (F20.9), two hospitalizations in past 18 months secondary to medication non-adherence with oral antipsychotics. Currently on oral aripiprazole 15mg daily — adequate symptom control when adherent. Psychiatry documents ongoing non-adherence pattern (missed doses >50% per pill count). PANSS score 62 at last visit. Long-acting injectable requested to address adherence barrier. No metabolic contraindications. Weight stable.

61-year-old male with Type 2 diabetes (E11.9). HbA1c 7.8%. Currently on metformin 1000mg BID. Requesting dulaglutide. No documented failure of SGLT-2 inhibitor. No documented cardiovascular disease, CKD, or heart failure. Physician note states patient 'prefers injectable.' BMI 29.1.

43-year-old female with Guillain-Barré syndrome, relapsing form / CIDP confirmed (G61.0). Nerve conduction study 01/2024 confirms demyelinating polyneuropathy. Neurology at academic medical center recommends IVIG maintenance therapy. Failed corticosteroids — prednisone 60mg daily x 8 weeks, inadequate response and significant weight gain (+22 lbs). Failed plasmapheresis x 5 sessions — partial response only, relapsed within 4 weeks. Functional status MRC sum score 42/60. IgA level normal (rules out IgA deficiency contraindication).

38-year-old male with moderate-to-severe Crohn's disease (K50.10). Harvey-Bradshaw Index 12. Failed mesalamine — not appropriate for Crohn's per GI note. Failed azathioprine 2.5mg/kg x 5 months (leukopenia, WBC 2.8). Failed adalimumab 160/80/40mg — secondary loss of response after 14 months, fecal calprotectin rising to 1840 ug/g. Gastroenterology recommends vedolizumab given gut-selective mechanism and prior TNF failure. CRP 18.2. Colonoscopy 01/2024: deep ulcerations ileum and right colon.

68-year-old male with BPH (N40.1) and urinary retention (R33.8). PSA 6.8 ng/mL (age-adjusted borderline). Requesting CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast (CPT 74178). No urology referral. Requesting CT rather than ultrasound renal/bladder which is first-line for BPH evaluation. No prior imaging. Urodynamic testing not completed.

54-year-old female with metastatic breast cancer (C50.912), BRCA1 germline pathogenic variant confirmed (Z15.01). ER+/HER2-negative. Prior lines: letrozole (progression), palbociclib + fulvestrant (progression at 14 months). Oncology requesting olaparib as third-line based on OlympiAD trial data. ECOG 1. Creatinine 0.9, adequate marrow reserve (ANC 2.1, Hgb 10.8). Genetic counseling completed.

44-year-old female with moderate MDD (F33.1). PHQ-9 score 14. On sertraline 100mg — first antidepressant trial, 6 weeks in. Requesting TMS immediately. No prior augmentation strategies tried. No referral to psychiatry. No documented contraindications to continuing pharmacotherapy.

36-year-old male with severe plaque psoriasis (L40.0). PASI 22, DLQI 19, BSA 40%. Failed methotrexate 20mg weekly x 12 weeks — hepatotoxicity (LFTs 2.8x ULN). Failed narrowband UVB phototherapy x 24 sessions — inadequate response, PASI unchanged. Failed adalimumab 40mg q2w x 20 weeks — loss of response, PASI rebounded from 9 to 19. Dermatology at academic center supports IL-17A inhibitor as third-line. Negative TB, no active infections.

47-year-old female with morbid obesity (E66.01, BMI 41.2), hypertension (I10), and hyperlipidemia (E78.5). Requesting semaglutide 2.4mg for chronic weight management. Completed 6-month lifestyle intervention program (documented monthly visits). BMI qualifies at 41.2 (threshold ≥30 with comorbidity or ≥40 without). Two comorbidities present. No prior GLP-1 agonist for obesity. Endocrinology supportive.

28-year-old male with paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria (D59.5) confirmed by flow cytometry (clone size 72%). LDH 4.8x ULN. Two thrombotic events in past 18 months (DVT, portal vein thrombosis). On anticoagulation. Hematology at tertiary center recommends eculizumab. Meningococcal vaccination series completed. No prior complement inhibitor therapy — eculizumab is first-line per PNH guidelines.

42-year-old female requesting screening bilateral diagnostic mammogram (CPT 77067). No symptoms, no palpable mass, no family history of breast cancer, no prior abnormal mammogram. Last screening mammogram 8 months ago — reported as BI-RADS 1 (negative). Requesting repeat screening before annual interval. No documented clinical indication for early repeat screening.

61-year-old female with seropositive RA (M05.79), RF 210 IU/mL, anti-CCP 380 U/mL. DAS28 5.9. Failed methotrexate 25mg weekly x 8 months (inadequate response). Failed leflunomide 20mg daily x 6 months (inadequate response, DAS28 5.7). Failed adalimumab 40mg q2w x 12 months — primary non-response (DAS28 never below 5.1). Rheumatology recommends T-cell costimulation inhibitor given TNF primary non-responder status. Negative TB, hepatitis B/C. No active infections.

39-year-old male with obesity (E66.09, BMI 37.8) and alcohol use disorder in remission >12 months (F10.20). Requesting Contrave for weight management. Psychiatry confirms AUD remission, no active cravings, stable on naltrexone 50mg oral (now requesting to transition to combination product). Endocrinology supportive. No opioid use. No seizure history. Blood pressure 128/82. Liver enzymes WNL.
